schema_status: ACTIVE-V0 — P56 verifier reads this as the install-path contract; P57 will migrate the install rows to quality/catalogs/install-paths.json with the unified schema (id/dimension/cadence/kind/sources/verifier/artifact/status/freshness_ttl/waiver/blast_radius/owner_hint), the docs/tutorial/benchmark rows to quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible.json
purpose: Enumerate every command/snippet a documentation reader is told to run, with a verifier that simulates a fresh user. Closes the gap between scripts/end-state.py (file-shape invariants, crates.io max_version) and reality (does the URL return bytes? does the install script actually put a binary on PATH?).

lineage.fixes_class_of_miss: documented commands silently break when release pipeline / external surface drifts. v0.11.x curl-installer-asset regression is the canonical example.

field_glossary.id: stable slug; never renumber, never reuse
field_glossary.sources: every file:line where this snippet appears; if the snippet is duplicated across docs, ONE row, MANY sources
field_glossary.category: install | tutorial-step | quickstart-snippet | benchmark-claim | troubleshooting-recipe | helper-cmd
field_glossary.persona: user persona this snippet targets — drives which OS/image the verifier needs
field_glossary.command: the literal string the doc tells the user to paste (multi-line allowed)
field_glossary.preconditions: what the doc assumes is true before this command (curl on PATH, internet, crates.io reachable, prior step succeeded)
field_glossary.expected_outcome: asserts a verifier checks AFTER the command runs; written as concrete shell predicates
field_glossary.verifier: how to validate without trusting the agent's word — kind, command/script, container image, network requirement, timeout
field_glossary.artifact: path under .planning/verifications/repro/<id>.json the verifier writes (mirrors §0.8 pattern)
field_glossary.last_verified_at: RFC3339 UTC of last verifier run
field_glossary.failure_mode: if FAIL, the diagnosed root cause (one sentence)
field_glossary.remediation_hint: where the FIX likely lives (file + glob)
field_glossary.blast_radius: P0 (every user hits) | P1 (one persona) | P2 (rare path)

verification_kinds.asset-existence: HEAD/GET against a URL; assert HTTP 200 AND content-length > N AND first bytes match a magic string
verification_kinds.container-rehearsal: spin a fresh container (ubuntu:24.04, alpine:3.19, etc.), run the snippet verbatim, assert post-conditions; this is THE 'simulate a fresh user' verifier
verification_kinds.shell-against-checkout: run snippet against the current git checkout; cheap, suitable for build-from-source
verification_kinds.asset-checksum: fetch the URL, compute sha256, assert it matches a pinned digest (drift detector)
verification_kinds.snippet-extraction-only: parse the doc, assert the snippet still exists at the claimed file:line (drift detector for renames/edits)
verification_kinds.manual: human-only — flag in catalog, never auto-graded GREEN

asserts: GET https://github.com/reubenjohn/reposix/releases/latest/download/reposix-installer.sh returns HTTP 200
the response body is >= 1024 bytes and starts with '#!'
in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container with curl installed, the documented one-liner exits 0
after the one-liner, `command -v reposix` exits 0
after the one-liner, `command -v git-remote-reposix` exits 0
`reposix --version` prints the latest workspace version (matches the GH release tag's version component)

claims.failure_mode: Latest GitHub Release (`reposix-cli-v0.11.2`) has assets:[]. The release.yml workflow trigger is tag glob `v*`, but release-plz now creates per-crate tags like `reposix-cli-v0.11.2` which do not match. release.yml has not run since v0.11.0 (2026-04-26 workflow_dispatch).
claims.remediation_hint: P56 Wave B fixes .github/workflows/release.yml to fire on `reposix-cli-v*` tags (Option A); pivot to Option B (release-plz mirror tag) if Option A breaks the version derivation. See .planning/research/v0.12.0-install-regression-diagnosis.md.

asserts: GET https://github.com/reubenjohn/reposix/releases/latest/download/reposix-installer.ps1 returns HTTP 200
the response body is >= 1024 bytes
container-rehearsal deferred to v0.12.1 windows runner; for v0.12.0 the asset-existence + min-bytes check is the contract

verifier.ci_friendly: asset-existence yes; container-rehearsal only on windows-2022 GH runner

claims.failure_mode: Same root cause as install/curl-installer-sh — release.yml doesn't fire on per-crate release-plz tags.
claims.remediation_hint: P56 Wave B fixes .github/workflows/release.yml to fire on `reposix-cli-v*` tags (Option A); pivot to Option B (release-plz mirror tag) if Option A breaks the version derivation. See .planning/research/v0.12.0-install-regression-diagnosis.md.

asserts: in a fresh rust:1.82-slim container with cargo-binstall installed, `cargo binstall --no-confirm reposix-cli reposix-remote` exits 0

stdout/stderr show binstall resolving to a prebuilt GitHub release binary, NOT a source-build fallback (assert by greppping for 'Downloading' against a github.com/reubenjohn/reposix/releases/download URL; failure mode: assert FAILS if the log contains the string 'Falling back' or 'compiling reposix' against a crates.io source path)
after install, `command -v reposix` and `command -v git-remote-reposix` both exit 0
`reposix --version` matches the latest crates.io max_version of reposix-cli

verifier.note: binstall will fall back to source-build when no GH binary asset exists. Verifier should warn (not fail) on fallback so we know the fast path regressed.

claims.failure_mode: binstall resolves but currently falls back to source-build because release.yml hasn't published binary assets — same root cause as curl-installer-sh; fix lifts this to PASS-fast-path.
claims.remediation_hint: P56 Wave B fixes .github/workflows/release.yml to fire on `reposix-cli-v*` tags (Option A); pivot to Option B (release-plz mirror tag) if Option A breaks the version derivation. See .planning/research/v0.12.0-install-regression-diagnosis.md.

asserts: GET https://api.github.com/repos/reubenjohn/homebrew-reposix/contents/Formula/reposix.rb returns HTTP 200
the formula's `version "X.Y.Z"` string equals the latest reposix-cli release tag's version component (parsed from the GH release tag, e.g. reposix-cli-v0.11.3 → 0.11.3)
the formula's three sha256 strings are non-empty 64-hex-char strings (no template literals or empty quotes)
container-rehearsal deferred to v0.12.1 macos runner per cost-conscious decision; for v0.12.0 the formula-version check is the contract

verifier.ci_friendly: tap reachability yes; brew install only on macos-14 GH runner
verifier.note: release.yml has an upload-homebrew-formula job; without release.yml runs the tap formula goes stale.

claims.failure_mode: Formula version likely pinned at 0.11.0 because upload-homebrew-formula is a release.yml job that hasn't run since v0.11.0. Confirm with `gh api repos/reubenjohn/homebrew-reposix/contents/Formula/reposix.rb`.
claims.remediation_hint: P56 Wave B fixes .github/workflows/release.yml to fire on `reposix-cli-v*` tags (Option A); pivot to Option B (release-plz mirror tag) if Option A breaks the version derivation. See .planning/research/v0.12.0-install-regression-diagnosis.md.

claims.command: git clone https://github.com/reubenjohn/reposix && cd reposix && cargo build --release --workspace --bins && export PATH="$PWD/target/release:$PATH"

asserts: in the current git checkout, `cargo build --release --workspace --bins` exits 0
target/release/reposix exists and is executable
target/release/git-remote-reposix exists and is executable
covered by ci.yml `test` job — no separate Wave D verifier action needed

verifier.ci_friendly: yes — already covered by ci.yml `test` job which builds the workspace

claims.last_verified_at: implicit via ci.yml `test` job last green run

preconditions: install/build-from-source OR install/cargo-binstall OR install/curl-installer-sh succeeded

asserts: step 2: simulator starts and listens on 127.0.0.1:7878
step 3: `reposix init sim::demo /tmp/repo` exits 0 and configures partial-clone
step 4: `git checkout -B main refs/reposix/origin/main` exits 0
step 5: issues/0001.md exists with `title:` frontmatter
step 7: `git push` reports `[new branch] main -> main`
step 8: `audit_events_cache` has a row with `op LIKE 'helper_push_%'`

verifier.note: Already exists. Run it; promote to ci.yml as a job.

claims.failure_mode: not in CI — silent bit-rot risk. Script's own header notes this: 'CI wiring: NOT yet wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml'.
claims.remediation_hint: .github/workflows/ci.yml — add `tutorial-repro` job that runs `bash scripts/repro-quickstart.sh` after `test` job
claims.blast_radius: P0 — tutorial is the headline conversion path

claims.command: cd /tmp/reposix-demo && sed -i 's/^status: .*/status: in_progress/' issues/0001.md && echo $'\n## Comment\nReproduced — investigating root cause.' >> issues/0001.md && git commit -am '0001: in progress' && git push
preconditions: tutorial/first-run-end-to-end through step 5 (a clone exists at /tmp/reposix-demo)

asserts: git push reports success
subsequent `git log -1 --format=%s` shows the commit message

verifier.note: Different working dir (/tmp/reposix-demo) than the tutorial (/tmp/repo). Real-user pitfall: the doc mixes two paths. Verifier should pin one and the docs should match.

claims.failure_mode: doc has a path inconsistency (/tmp/repo in tutorial vs /tmp/reposix-demo in index hero). Friction for a copy-paste user.
claims.remediation_hint: harmonize the path across docs/index.md, README.md, and docs/tutorials/first-run.md

claims.command: reposix sim --bind 127.0.0.1:7878 & && reposix init sim::demo /tmp/reposix-demo && cd /tmp/reposix-demo && git checkout -B main refs/reposix/origin/main && ls issues/ && sed -i 's/TODO/DONE/' issues/0001.md && git commit -am 'mark issue 1 done' && git push

asserts: overlaps with tutorial/first-run-end-to-end

claims.remediation_hint: either fold this into repro-quickstart.sh as a second mode, or assert this snippet is BYTE-IDENTICAL to a tested fixture

claims.command: <benchmark not directly user-runnable; numbers come from docs/benchmarks/latency.md>

asserts: docs/benchmarks/latency.md frontmatter `last_measured_at` is < 30 days old
the cached-read p50 cell value matches the headline `8 ms` to within ±2ms

verifier.note: Already cron'd via .github/workflows/bench-latency-cron.yml. Add a check that the headline number matches.

claims.failure_mode: no automated check that hero numbers in index/README match the bench output
claims.remediation_hint: add an end-state.py claim that greps the headline numbers from index.md/README.md and asserts they match the latency.md table

asserts: headline `89.1%` is greppable from docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md
the comparison fixture file referenced from token-economy.md exists

claims.failure_mode: same as 8ms claim — no cross-check between hero copy and bench output
claims.remediation_hint: single end-state.py claim per headline number that greps both surfaces

preconditions: a clone where blob-limit was hit

asserts: recovery succeeds; subsequent fetch under the limit works

layer_1_drift_detector.what: scripts/extract-doc-snippets.py — parse README.md, docs/index.md, docs/tutorials/*.md for fenced code blocks; emit JSON of (file, line, lang, content). Compare against the catalog's `sources`+`command` fields. Fail if a doc snippet has no catalog row, or a catalog row's content drifted from its source.
layer_1_drift_detector.wires_into: pre-push hook + ci.yml as a fast (<5s) check
layer_1_drift_detector.catches: doc edits that change a documented command without updating the verifier

layer_2_asset_existence.what: scripts/check-doc-urls.py — for every catalog row of kind asset-existence, HEAD/GET the URL, assert min_bytes + magic_prefix. Run nightly + on docs/release-workflow changes.
layer_2_asset_existence.wires_into: new .github/workflows/docs-repro.yml (cron + on push to release.yml/docs/**) — same shape as bench-latency-cron.yml
layer_2_asset_existence.catches: exactly the install/curl-installer-sh regression. Would have failed within 24h of the v0.11.1 release-plz cutover.

layer_3_container_rehearsal.what: scripts/run-doc-rehearsal.sh <id> — for catalog rows with kind container-rehearsal, spin a fresh ubuntu/alpine container, run the documented snippet verbatim, assert post-conditions. Persist log + outcome to .planning/verifications/repro/<id>.json.
layer_3_container_rehearsal.wires_into: .github/workflows/docs-repro.yml as a separate job matrix per persona (linux/mac/windows). Manual-trigger + nightly. NOT pre-push (too slow).
layer_3_container_rehearsal.catches: everything layer 2 doesn't — script content errors, env-var assumptions, path inconsistencies (e.g. the /tmp/repo vs /tmp/reposix-demo split flagged in quickstart/index-after-one-commit)

automation_proposal.tie_in_with_session_end_state: Add a single claim to SESSION-END-STATE.json: `docs/all-snippets-verified` whose verifier is `python3 scripts/end-state-from-repro.py` — reads docs_reproducible_catalog.json and asserts every row is PASS or has an explicit `accepted_failure: true` waiver. That hooks the catalog into the existing pre-push verdict gate.

next_actions_for_owner_review: Approve schema (this file). Suggest adding fields if useful — e.g. `accepted_failure_until: <date>` for known-broken rows we don't want to block on.

Decide on owner: this catalog is a planning artifact (.planning/) but the verifier scripts are code. Open a /gsd-quick to (a) build layer-1 drift-detector and (b) fix release.yml so install/curl-installer-sh flips to PASS.
Layer-3 container rehearsal is real money on CI. Recommend nightly cron + manual workflow_dispatch, NOT per-PR.
Three rows worth adding to SESSION-END-STATE.json TODAY (no extra infra needed): asset-existence checks for the curl/ps1 installer URLs and the homebrew tap formula version. Five lines of shell each.
